entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop studied for hybrid performance and impact on farmers.

entities.name: HPRC HYBRIDS
entities.description: Hybrids developed by the Pearl Millet Hybrid Parents Research Consortium between 2000-2010.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area, which increased by at least 20% with HPRC hybrids.

entities.name: FODDER YIELD
entities.description: The amount of fodder produced per unit area, which increased by at least 20% with HPRC hybrids.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the impact study of pearl millet hybrids was conducted.

entities.name: RAJASTHAN
entities.description: One of the three Indian states covered in the impact study.

entities.name: GUJARAT
entities.description: One of the three Indian states covered in the impact study.

entities.name: UTTAR PRADESH
entities.description: One of the three Indian states covered in the impact study.

entities.name: THIRD-PARTY EVALUATION
entities.description: The assessment method used in 2015 to evaluate the on-farm impact of HPRC hybrids.

entities.name: LIVELIHOODS
entities.description: The socio-economic well-being of farmers impacted by the adoption of HPRC hybrids.

relationships.description: HPRC hybrids are specific varieties of pearl millet
HPRC hybrids provided at least 20% higher grain yield
HPRC hybrids provided at least 20% higher fodder yield
Used to assess the on-farm impact of HPRC hybrids
Deployed and evaluated across various states in India
Rajasthan is a state located in India
Gujarat is a state located in India
Uttar Pradesh is a state located in India
HPRC hybrids were grown and evaluated in Rajasthan
HPRC hybrids were grown and evaluated in Gujarat
HPRC hybrids were grown and evaluated in Uttar Pradesh
HPRC hybrids positively impacted the livelihoods of farmers
